airbnb.pricingMarkup: ~5% above website base price. Airbnb is the preferred platform — higher quality leads, better differentiation, most volume by design.

rules: All guest communication must go through the Airbnb messaging system until booking is confirmed
After booking confirmation, hosts may share direct contact information (phone, email) within the platform message thread
Never ask guests to communicate outside the platform before a booking is confirmed
Messages are monitored for policy violations — assume all messages are reviewed
Automated messages are allowed but must comply with all content policies
Do not send marketing or promotional content unrelated to the current booking
Keep messages relevant to the guest's trip and booking
restrictions: No sharing of personal contact information (phone, email, WhatsApp) before booking confirmation
No directing guests to book off-platform or through a personal website
No mentioning that prices are cheaper if booked directly
No sharing links to competing booking platforms
No discriminatory language or content that violates anti-discrimination policies
No threatening, harassing, or abusive language
No requesting payments outside the Airbnb payment system
No requesting guests leave reviews or offering incentives for reviews
No sharing other guests' personal information
messaging.automatedMessageLimits: No hard limit on automated messages, but excessive messaging can lead to guest complaints. Best practice: limit to 4-5 automated touchpoints per booking (confirmation, pre-arrival, check-in day, mid-stay for longer stays, checkout reminder).
messaging.responseTimeRequirements: Respond to all new enquiries and booking requests within 24 hours to maintain response rate. Aim for under 1 hour — Superhost criteria requires 90%+ response rate. Response rate is measured on first response to new message threads, not every message. Pre-approval or declining a request also counts as a response.
messaging.responseTimeImpact: Response rate directly affects search ranking, Superhost eligibility, and the 'Responds within X' badge on your listing. Consistently slow responses can suppress listing visibility.

hostPolicies.name: Flexible
hostPolicies.description: Full refund up to 24 hours before check-in (except fees). Partial refund if cancelled less than 24 hours before.

hostPolicies.name: Moderate
hostPolicies.description: Full refund up to 5 days before check-in. 50% refund of nightly rate if cancelled less than 5 days before.

hostPolicies.name: Strict
hostPolicies.description: Full refund within 48 hours of booking if at least 14 days before check-in. 50% refund of nightly rate if cancelled at least 7 days before check-in. No refund after that.

hostPolicies.name: Super Strict 30 Days
hostPolicies.description: By invitation only. 50% refund up to 30 days before check-in. No refund within 30 days.

hostCancellationPenalties: Hosts who cancel confirmed bookings face severe penalties
First cancellation: calendar blocked for dates, $50 penalty fee, automated review noting cancellation
Repeated cancellations: $100 penalty fee per cancellation, Superhost status revoked, potential listing suspension or removal
Host cancellation rate affects search ranking and Superhost eligibility
Never cancel on a guest — contact Airbnb support to have the guest cancel if needed
guestPolicies: Guests receive refunds according to the listing's cancellation policy
Service fees are generally non-refundable if cancelled after 48 hours of booking
Guests can request a full refund within 24 hours of check-in if the listing is materially different from the description
cancellation.extenuatingCircumstances: Airbnb may override the cancellation policy for natural disasters, government travel restrictions, serious illness/injury (documented), death of host/guest or immediate family, or military deployment. Both host and guest can apply. Documentation required.

reviews.timeline: Both guest and host have 14 days after checkout to leave a review. Reviews are published simultaneously when both parties submit, or after 14 days, whichever comes first. Neither party can see the other's review before it's published.
policies: Reviews must reflect the actual experience during the stay
Hosts cannot offer incentives for positive reviews (discounts, gifts, refund promises)
Hosts cannot threaten negative reviews to pressure guests
Retaliatory reviews (reviewing based on the review you received) violate policy and can be removed
Reviews mentioning Airbnb policies or pricing rather than the stay experience may be removed
Guests cannot use review threats to get refunds or policy exceptions
responseGuidelines: You have 30 days after a review is published to post a public response
Responses are public and visible to all future guests — write for your future audience, not the reviewer
Keep responses professional, factual, and brief (2-4 sentences)
For positive reviews: thank the guest, mention something specific about their stay
For negative reviews: acknowledge the issue without being defensive, state what you've done to fix it, invite future guests to experience the improvement
Never argue, blame, or get personal in review responses
Never reveal private details about the guest's stay
A thoughtful response to a negative review builds more trust than no negative reviews at all
reviews.superhostReviewRequirements: Maintain 4.8+ overall rating with at least 10 reviews in the past year (or 100+ nights with 3+ stays).

resolutionCenter.process: Use Airbnb's Resolution Centre to request money from guests for damages or extra charges. Hosts should submit a request through the Resolution Centre, and if the guest declines, Airbnb can mediate. Always document damage with timestamped photos and provide evidence of the pre-stay condition.
resolutionCenter.damageClaimTimeline: Submit damage claims within 14 days of checkout or before the next guest checks in, whichever is sooner. Late claims are typically denied. Take photos/video before and after every stay.
tips: Always photograph damage immediately upon discovery with timestamps
Maintain a baseline photo set of the property (pre-stay walkthrough)
Include receipts or quotes for repairs/replacement
Contact Airbnb Support if the guest is unresponsive after 72 hours
AirCover for Hosts provides up to $3M in damage protection — but you must document and report promptly
Keep communication about damages in the Airbnb message thread for evidence

contentRestrictions: No sharing personal phone numbers, email addresses, or social media before booking is confirmed
No directing guests to book off-platform
No mentioning lower prices on other platforms or direct booking sites
No discriminatory preferences or language (race, religion, nationality, gender, etc.)
No sexually explicit or inappropriate content in messages or listings
No misleading information about the property or amenities
No requesting cash payments or payments outside Airbnb
No promoting illegal activities
No sharing other properties' listing URLs in guest messages (can appear as redirecting off-platform)
Photos must accurately represent the current state of the property
hostObligations: Provide accurate listing information — description, photos, amenities must reflect reality
Maintain the property in safe, habitable condition
Respond to guest messages in a timely manner
Provide all essential amenities as listed (towels, linens, toilet paper, soap, hot water)
Comply with local laws, regulations, and tax obligations
Disclose any security cameras or monitoring devices (cameras only allowed in common areas, never in bedrooms or bathrooms)
Provide accurate check-in instructions before arrival
Be available or have a co-host available for guest issues during stays
penaltyTriggers: Host-initiated cancellations of confirmed bookings ($50-$100+ fee, calendar blocked, Superhost at risk)
Response rate below 90% (affects search ranking and Superhost status)
Repeated guest complaints about cleanliness or accuracy (listing suppressed in search)
Requesting off-platform payments (listing removal, potential ban)
Review manipulation or incentivised reviews (review removal, potential suspension)
Safety incidents that are not promptly addressed
Multiple valid damage claims indicating negligence
Rating dropping below 4.0 (risk of listing deactivation)

criteria: At least 10 trips OR 100 nights across at least 3 stays in the past year
benefits: Superhost badge on listing and profile
Increased guest trust and booking rates
Eligible for Superhost-only bonuses and promotions
superhost.assessmentPeriod: Reviewed quarterly (January 1, April 1, July 1, October 1). Based on the prior 365 days of activity.

bookingcom.pricingMarkup: 10-20% above website base price depending on Genius status. Genius discounts: Level 1 = 10%, Level 2 = 15%, Level 3 = 20% — base price marked up higher to compensate.

rules: All pre-arrival communication should go through the Booking.com Extranet messaging or Partner Hub
Hosts can share contact details (phone, email) in the property description and confirmation emails
Booking.com is more permissive than Airbnb about direct contact — guests see the host's phone number and email after booking
Templates can be set up for automated messages at different stages
Messages should be professional and relevant to the booking
restrictions: Do not request guests cancel and rebook on another platform
Do not solicit off-platform bookings for future stays in messages
Do not send marketing emails to Booking.com guests without consent
Payment must go through Booking.com's system (or Virtual Credit Card if applicable)
No discriminatory content in any communication
Do not request reviews or offer incentives for reviews
messaging.responseTimeRequirements: Respond to all guest messages within 24 hours. Booking.com tracks response time as part of your property score. Aim for under 4 hours for Quick Response badge.
messaging.replyScoreImpact: Reply speed and rate contribute to your property's internal quality score, which affects search ranking. Properties with faster response times appear higher in search results. Booking.com may display 'Quick responder' or similar badges.

cancellation.freeCancellationExpectations: Booking.com heavily promotes free cancellation options. Properties offering free cancellation get significantly more visibility. Consider offering free cancellation up to 7-14 days before check-in to balance conversion rates with protection.
cancellation.noShowPolicies: For no-shows, charge up to the first night (or per your policy). Report no-shows in the Extranet within 48 hours. Booking.com may waive guest charges in some force majeure situations.
cancellation.modificationRules: Guests can request modifications through Booking.com. Hosts are not obligated to accept modifications but refusing frequently can impact scores. Date changes, guest count changes, and room type changes can be handled through the Extranet.
hostCancellationPenalties: Cancellation by host is strongly penalised on Booking.com
Each host cancellation triggers a commission surcharge on future bookings
Repeated cancellations lead to listing suppression or suspension
Calendar may be automatically blocked for cancelled dates
If you must cancel, relocate the guest to an equivalent or better property and report through Extranet

reviews.timeline: Guests receive a review invitation email from Booking.com within 48 hours after checkout. They have 90 days to submit a review. Hosts cannot initiate the review process — it is automated by Booking.com.
scoringFactors: Sub-categories: Staff, Facilities, Cleanliness, Comfort, Value for money, Location, Free WiFi
The overall score is the average of sub-category scores
Booking.com weights recent reviews more heavily in the displayed score
A minimum of 5 reviews is needed before a score is displayed
responseGuidelines: Respond through the Extranet — both public and private responses are available
Public responses are visible to all potential guests
Keep responses professional, warm, and forward-looking
For negative reviews: acknowledge, don't argue, state improvements made
Booking.com may remove reviews that contain profanity, personal attacks, or content unrelated to the stay
You cannot remove genuine reviews — focus on improving the experience
reviews.targetScore: Aim for 8.0+ for good visibility. 9.0+ earns 'Superb' badge. Scores below 7.0 significantly reduce search visibility.

payments.payoutTimeline: Booking.com pays hosts via bank transfer. Standard payout is after guest checkout, typically received within 1-2 weeks depending on your bank and region. Some properties use Virtual Credit Cards (VCC) which are charged at check-in.
payments.virtualCreditCards: Booking.com may issue Virtual Credit Cards (VCCs) for guest payments. VCCs are pre-loaded with the booking amount and can be charged at check-in or after. Some properties prefer Payments by Booking.com, where the platform handles collection and pays the host directly.
payments.commissionStructure: Standard commission is 15% of the booking subtotal (before taxes/fees). Commission percentage may vary by region and property type. Commission is only charged on completed stays, not on valid cancellations.

factors: Reply speed and response rate to messages
Cancellation rate by host (lower is better)
optimizationTips: Keep calendar up to date — close dates you cannot honour rather than cancelling bookings
Respond to all messages within 6 hours
Complete every field in your property listing
Offer flexible cancellation for at least some rate plans
Keep pricing competitive — use the rate insights tool in Extranet
Participate in Genius programme for additional visibility
Offer mobile-only rates for incremental bookings
Maintain cleanliness and comfort scores above 8.5

contentRestrictions: Property descriptions must be accurate and up to date
Photos must accurately represent the property
Do not redirect guests to book on other platforms
No discriminatory language or policies
Pricing must include all mandatory fees (no hidden charges)
Must comply with local laws and regulations
Cannot request tips or surcharges not listed in the rate

geniusProgram.description: Genius is Booking.com's loyalty programme that rewards frequent travellers with discounts and perks. Properties can opt in to offer Genius rates (10-20% discount) in exchange for increased visibility to high-value travellers.
levels: Genius Level 1: 10% discount, free breakfast or room upgrade where applicable
Genius Level 3: 20% discount + priority support + free room upgrades
geniusProgram.hostBenefits: Participating hosts get a Genius badge, increased search visibility among Genius travellers, and access to a larger pool of loyal repeat bookers. The discounted rate comes from the host's margin.
geniusProgram.consideration: Evaluate whether the increased booking volume from Genius visibility offsets the margin reduction. Works well for properties with lower occupancy that need volume.

preferredPartner.description: Preferred Partner Programme increases visibility in exchange for a higher commission rate (typically 17-18% instead of 15%).
preferredPartner.consideration: Calculate whether the increased bookings justify the higher commission. Most useful during low seasons or for new listings building traction.

rules: All initial communication should go through Vrbo's messaging system
Host contact details are shared with guests after booking
Respond promptly to enquiries — Vrbo tracks response metrics
Messages should be relevant to the booking and property
restrictions: No soliciting off-platform bookings before a booking is confirmed
No requesting payments outside Vrbo's system
No sharing links to competing platforms
messaging.responseTimeRequirements: Respond within 24 hours. Vrbo tracks response rate and time, which affects search ranking. Aim for under 6 hours for best visibility.
messaging.responseTimeImpact: Properties with faster response times and higher response rates receive better search placement and a 'Responds quickly' badge.

hostPolicies.name: No refund
hostPolicies.description: No refund for cancellation at any time.

hostPolicies.name: Strict
hostPolicies.description: Full refund if cancelled 60+ days before check-in. 50% refund if 30-60 days before. No refund within 30 days.

hostPolicies.name: Firm
hostPolicies.description: Full refund if cancelled 60+ days before check-in. 50% refund if 30-60 days. No refund within 30 days.

hostPolicies.name: Moderate
hostPolicies.description: Full refund if cancelled 30+ days before check-in. 50% refund if 14-30 days. No refund within 14 days.

hostPolicies.name: Relaxed
hostPolicies.description: Full refund if cancelled 14+ days before check-in. 50% refund if 7-14 days. No refund within 7 days.

hostCancellationPenalties: Host cancellations result in penalties and listing suppression
Repeated cancellations can lead to listing removal

reviews.timeline: Guests can leave reviews for up to 1 year after checkout on Vrbo. Review invitations are sent by email after checkout.
policies: Reviews must reflect the actual stay experience
Sub-categories include cleanliness, communication, check-in, location, and listing accuracy
Reviews can be reported for policy violations but genuine reviews cannot be removed
responseGuidelines: For negative reviews: acknowledge, state improvement, invite return

payments.payoutTimeline: Vrbo pays hosts via direct deposit, typically within 1-2 business days after guest check-in.
payments.serviceFees: Vrbo charges either a per-booking service fee to the guest (typically 6-12%) or a subscription model for the host ($499/year per listing). Most hosts use the per-booking model.
payments.commissionStructure: If using the per-booking model, Vrbo charges 5% commission to the host plus 6-12% service fee to the guest. If using subscription, only the annual fee applies.

contentRestrictions: Accurate property descriptions and photos required
No discriminatory content or policies
Must disclose all fees upfront
No misleading amenity claims
Security camera disclosure required

criteria: Maintain a booking acceptance rate above 90%

direct.depositRequirements: For direct bookings, always collect a security deposit (typically 20-30% of total stay or a fixed amount based on property value). Clarify deposit terms, refund conditions, and timeline in writing before accepting payment.
direct.contractTerms: Always use a rental agreement for direct bookings. Include: guest names, dates, property address, total cost breakdown, cancellation policy, house rules, damage liability, check-in/checkout times, maximum occupancy, and governing law/jurisdiction.
direct.idVerification: Request government-issued ID from the primary guest for direct bookings. Verify the name matches the payment method. Store ID information securely and in compliance with data protection laws (GDPR if applicable).
paymentMethods: Bank transfer (preferred — lower fees, clear paper trail)
Credit card via payment processor (Stripe, Square — adds 2-3% fee but provides chargeback protection)
PayPal (acceptable but higher fees and chargeback risk)
Never accept cash for the full booking amount — always have a documented payment trail
Wire transfer for international bookings (verify bank details carefully — wire fraud is common)
direct.noPlatformProtection: Direct bookings have no platform mediation or host protection programme. You are responsible for your own damage protection, payment disputes, and guest vetting. Consider requiring travel insurance and maintaining your own short-term rental insurance policy.
advantages: No commission fees (save 15-20% vs. OTA bookings)
Direct relationship with guest — can market future stays
Full control over pricing, policies, and communication
Can collect email addresses for marketing (with consent)
No platform policy changes to worry about
risks: No review system for social proof (unless using your own website)
Higher risk of payment fraud or chargebacks
Must handle all guest vetting and verification yourself
No damage protection programme — need private insurance
Legal liability falls entirely on the host
bestPractices: Use a channel manager to sync calendars with OTAs
Maintain your own terms and conditions reviewed by a local lawyer
Take pre- and post-stay photos for every booking
Collect signed rental agreements before arrival
Use a payment processor with fraud protection
Build a guest database for repeat bookings and referrals

priceParity.description: Most OTAs have rate parity clauses in their contracts, but enforcement varies by region and has been relaxed in the EU. Price parity means you cannot advertise a lower price on a competing platform.
priceParity.airbnb: Airbnb does not enforce strict rate parity — you can price differently on other platforms. However, significantly lower prices elsewhere may trigger Airbnb's price tips or Smart Pricing adjustments.
priceParity.bookingcom: Booking.com historically enforced rate parity (same or better price on their platform). In the EU, narrow rate parity clauses were banned in some countries (France, Germany, Italy, Austria). In practice, moderate price differences are tolerated but large discrepancies may affect visibility.
priceParity.vrbo: Vrbo does not strictly enforce rate parity. You can set different prices on different platforms.
priceParity.directBooking: You are free to offer lower direct booking prices. This is a common and accepted practice for building your direct channel.
priceParity.bestPractice: Price consistently across OTAs to avoid guest confusion and potential policy issues. Offer your best rates for direct bookings to incentivise your direct channel.

calendarSync.importance: Double bookings are one of the most damaging issues — they lead to host cancellations (severe penalties on all platforms), guest displacement, negative reviews, and potential platform suspension.
methods: iCal sync between platforms (standard, but has 15-60 minute delay)
Channel manager (PMS) for real-time sync (recommended for multi-platform hosts)
Manual calendar management (not recommended — high error risk)
calendarSync.bestPractice: Use a channel manager for real-time sync. If using iCal, set sync frequency to maximum (every 15 minutes) and maintain buffer days between bookings. Always block dates manually on all platforms immediately when accepting a booking before sync catches up.

contentConsistency.description: Maintain consistent property descriptions, amenity lists, and photos across all platforms. Guests often cross-reference listings on different platforms. Inconsistencies erode trust and can lead to complaints.
tips: Use the same professional photos on all platforms
Keep amenity lists identical — don't list a pool on Airbnb but not on Booking.com
Ensure house rules are consistent across platforms
Update all platforms simultaneously when making property changes
Adapt description tone slightly per platform audience but keep facts identical

airbnb.audienceProfile: Often younger, experience-oriented travellers. Expect personalised, friendly communication. Value local tips and unique experiences. Used to messaging within the app.
airbnb.toneExpectation: Warm, friendly, conversational. Personal touches appreciated. Guests often want to know about the host.
airbnb.communicationStyle: In-app messaging primary. Many guests prefer text-style communication. Quick responses expected.

bookingcom.audienceProfile: Broader demographic, includes more business travellers and hotel-experienced guests. Expect professional, efficient communication. May be less engaged in messaging.
bookingcom.toneExpectation: Professional and efficient. Clear, structured information. Less casual than Airbnb but still warm.
bookingcom.communicationStyle: Email-based communication more common. Guests may be less responsive to messages. Provide all essential information proactively.

vrbo.audienceProfile: Often families and groups booking larger properties for vacations. More likely to be experienced vacation rental users. Value detailed property information.
vrbo.toneExpectation: Professional, detailed, family-friendly. Emphasis on practical information — parking, nearby family activities, kitchen equipment.
vrbo.communicationStyle: Mix of in-app and email. Guests may have detailed questions about the property and surrounding area.

direct.audienceProfile: Often repeat guests or referrals. More likely to have a direct relationship with the host. May be more flexible and understanding.
direct.toneExpectation: Personal and relationship-focused. Can be more casual once rapport is established.
direct.communicationStyle: WhatsApp, email, or phone. More flexible and direct. Build the relationship for repeat bookings.

complianceChecklist.description: Use this checklist to validate any guest-facing message before sending.

checks.description: Never suggest, imply, or link to booking outside the current platform. This includes phrases like 'book direct next time', 'visit our website', or sharing booking links for other platforms.

checks.rule: No contact info before booking confirmation
checks.description: Do not share phone numbers, email addresses, WhatsApp, or social media handles with guests before their booking is confirmed on Airbnb.
Never ask guests to pay outside the platform's payment system. This includes cash, direct bank transfer, PayPal, or any other method not handled by the platform.
Do not offer discounts, gifts, upgrades, or any other incentive in exchange for reviews. Do not explicitly ask guests to leave a review — let the platform's automated review request handle it.
Ensure messages contain no language that could be interpreted as discriminatory based on race, ethnicity, nationality, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age, or disability.
Do not mention that your property is available at a different (especially lower) price on another platform or through direct booking.
Do not overstate amenities, views, distances, or features in messages. Everything stated in messages should match the listing and reality.

If the property has any cameras (even exterior-only), noise monitors, or other monitoring devices, ensure guests are informed. Airbnb requires disclosure in the listing and will suspend hosts who have undisclosed cameras.
Never share one guest's personal information, booking details, or review content with another guest.
